Key theological claims are intact: (1) 'das Evangelium zwingt uns' → 'az evangélium arra kényszerít minket' correctly conveys the compulsion of the Gospel; (2) 'durch Beobachtung menschlicher Traditionen können wir keine Gnade verdienen noch gerechtfertigt werden' → 'az emberi rendelések megtartásával nem tudunk kegyelmet érdemelni, sem megigazulni' correctly preserves both the merit and justification aspects; (3) Peter's speech in Acts 15:10-11 is fully rendered including the crucial soteriological statement 'we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they' → 'az Úr Jézus Krisztus kegyelme által hiszünk megtartatni, miképpen azok is'; (4) All Scripture citations (Matt. 15:3, 9; Rom. 14:17; Col. 2:16; Acts 15:10) are present and correctly rendered. The note about the tradition being 'not unlawful but indifferent' (közömbös dolog) is preserved. The condemnation of human traditions as unnecessary worship acts is fully intact.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All glossary terms are used consistently and correctly: 'Gnade' → 'kegyelem' (consistent throughout); 'Gerechtigkeit des Glaubens' → 'hit általi igazság' (correct per glossary); 'Menschensatzungen' → 'emberi rendelések' (correct); 'Reinigungen des Gesetzes' → 'törvényi tisztulások' (correct per glossary); 'Gnade des Herrn Jesu Christi' → 'az Úr Jézus Krisztus kegyelme' (correct per glossary); 'Reich Gottes' → 'Isten országa' (correct per glossary); 'Sabbat' → 'szombat' (correct per glossary); 'Kegyelemtan' (Gnadenlehre) → correct per glossary; 'Zeugnisse' → 'bizonyságtételek' (correct per glossary). The translation consistently uses established Hungarian theological vocabulary throughout.

clarity.explanation: The text is generally clear and well-structured. The use of archaic Károli Bible Hungarian ('terheltetitek magatokat', 'annakokáért', 'miképpen azok is', 'kísértitek') is contextually appropriate for a confessional text but may require slight effort from modern readers unfamiliar with classical Hungarian. The sentence 'amely azonban nyilvánvalóan nem tiltott, hanem közömbös dologra vonatkozik, és bizonyos rokonsága van a törvényi tisztulásokkal' is somewhat complex but still follows the source structure. One minor point: 'így szól, 9' (referring to verse 9) could be slightly clearer as 'a 9. versben így szól' but this does not materially obscure meaning.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads naturally in an elevated, confessional register appropriate for this type of theological document. The biblical quotations use traditional Károli Bible language ('Senki azért titeket meg ne ítéljen,' 'miért kísértitek az Istent'), which is appropriate and expected for this genre. Minor trace of translation: 'Tehát nem kíván haszontalan szolgálatot' is slightly terse as a standalone sentence but acceptable. The phrase 'Röviddel ezután hozzáteszi' is natural Hungarian. Overall the text reads as a competent theological translation in the expected formal register.

suggestions: The phrase 'Sőt inkább az Úr Jézus Krisztus kegyelme által hiszünk megtartatni' uses archaic Károli language ('megtartatni') which is appropriate for the register but ensure the target audience is familiar with this formulation.
The source text has a typographical error 'Jesus Ohrist' — the translation correctly renders this as 'Jézus Krisztus', which is appropriate.

The Col. 2 citation is somewhat expanded compared to the source ('Senki azért titeket meg ne ítéljen evésért, vagy ivásért, avagy ünnep, vagy újhold, vagy szombat dolgában') — the addition of 'újhold' (new moon) is from the full biblical text but not in the source passage as quoted. This is a minor observation and may be intentional for biblical fidelity.
The phrase 'efféle rendelésekkel' in the Colossians quotation could optionally be clarified with a footnote for modern readers, though the Károli rendering is doctrinally sound.

The translation of 'Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man' includes the completion 'hanem ami a szájon kijön, az fertőzteti meg az embert' — this addition (the second clause) goes slightly beyond the source text as quoted, though it reflects the full biblical verse. Consider whether to match the source text exactly or retain the fuller biblical quotation.
Consider rendering 'így szól, 9' more clearly as 'és a 9. versben így szól' to help readers follow the verse reference more naturally.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Denn das Evangelium zwingt uns dazu, in den Kirchen an der Gnadenlehre und der Gerechtigkeit durch den Glauben festzuhalten; welche jedoch nicht verstanden werden kann, wenn die Menschen meinen, dass sie durch Werke nach eigener Wahl Gnade verdienen.

So lehrten sie also, dass wir durch das Halten menschlicher Ordnungen keine Gnade verdienen können, noch gerechtfertigt werden; und daher darf man solche Werke nicht als notwendige Gottesdiensthandlungen betrachten. Dazu fügen sie auch die Zeugnisse der Heiligen Schrift hinzu. Christus, Matthäus 15, 3, verteidigt die Apostel, die die übliche Überlieferung nicht gehalten hatten, welche jedoch offensichtlich nicht auf etwas Verbotenes, sondern auf etwas Gleichgültiges sich bezieht und eine gewisse Verwandtschaft mit den gesetzlichen Reinigungen hat, und spricht so, 9: Sie ehren mich aber vergeblich, weil sie solche Lehren lehren, die Menschengebote sind. Also will er keinen unnützen Dienst. Kurz darauf fügt er hinzu: Nicht das verunreinigt den Menschen, was zum Mund hineingeht, sondern was zum Mund herauskommt, das verunreinigt den Menschen. Ebenso Paulus, Röm. 14, 17: Denn das Reich Gottes ist nicht Essen und Trinken, sondern Gerechtigkeit, Friede und Freude im Heiligen Geist. Kol. 2, 16: So lasst nun niemanden euch richten über Essen oder Trinken, oder über einen Festtag, oder Neumond, oder Sabbat: ferner: Wenn ihr nun mit Christus den Elementen der Welt abgestorben seid, warum lasst ihr euch noch als ob ihr in der Welt lebtet, mit solchen Ordnungen beschweren: Greife nicht an, koste nicht, rühre nicht an; Petrus aber spricht so, Apg. 15, 10: Warum versucht ihr nun Gott, dass ihr ein Joch auf den Hals der Jünger legt, welches weder unsere Väter noch wir haben tragen können? Sondern wir glauben vielmehr, durch die Gnade des Herrn Jesu Christi selig zu werden, gleichwie auch jene.

differences.original_phrase: by observances of their own choice
differences.back_translated_phrase: durch Werke nach eigener Wahl
differences.explanation: The original says 'observances' (religious/ritual practices), while the back-translation uses 'Werke' (works/deeds). This is a slight narrowing but does not change the doctrinal point being made about human merit.

differences.original_phrase: In vain do they worship Me with the commandments of men
differences.back_translated_phrase: Sie ehren mich aber vergeblich, weil sie solche Lehren lehren, die Menschengebote sind
differences.explanation: The back-translation expands the quotation with an explanatory clause ('weil sie solche Lehren lehren, die Menschengebote sind'), reflecting a fuller rendering of the Matthew 15:9 text. The doctrinal meaning is preserved.

differences.original_phrase: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man
differences.back_translated_phrase: Nicht das verunreinigt den Menschen, was zum Mund hineingeht, sondern was zum Mund herauskommt, das verunreinigt den Menschen
differences.explanation: The back-translation includes the second half of the verse ('sondern was zum Mund herauskommt'), which is not present in the original excerpt. This is an addition from the fuller biblical text but does not alter the doctrinal point.

differences.original_phrase: The kingdom of God is not meat and drink
differences.back_translated_phrase: Denn das Reich Gottes ist nicht Essen und Trinken, sondern Gerechtigkeit, Friede und Freude im Heiligen Geist
differences.explanation: The back-translation includes the continuation of Romans 14:17 ('sondern Gerechtigkeit, Friede und Freude im Heiligen Geist'), which was not in the original excerpt. This is an addition from the fuller biblical text but does not alter the doctrinal argument.

differences.original_phrase: or in respect of an holy-day
differences.back_translated_phrase: oder über einen Festtag, oder Neumond
differences.explanation: The back-translation adds 'oder Neumond' (or new moon), reflecting a fuller rendering of Colossians 2:16. This does not change the doctrinal point.

differences.original_phrase: But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they
differences.back_translated_phrase: Sondern wir glauben vielmehr, durch die Gnade des Herrn Jesu Christi selig zu werden, gleichwie auch jene
differences.explanation: The back-translation uses 'selig zu werden' (to be saved/blessed) which is a standard German rendering of 'saved.' The word 'vielmehr' (rather/even more so) adds a slight contrastive emphasis not in the original, but the doctrinal content is fully preserved.

differences.original_phrase: Jesus Ohrist
differences.back_translated_phrase: Jesu Christi
differences.explanation: The original has a typographical error ('Ohrist' instead of 'Christ'). The back-translation correctly renders this as 'Christi.' No doctrinal difference.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is fully preserved across both texts. The differences are all minor and consist of: (1) slightly fuller biblical quotations in the back-translation that include portions of the verses not cited in the original excerpt, (2) a synonym substitution ('Werke' for 'observances'), and (3) minor stylistic additions. No theological meaning has been lost or distorted.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation accurately preserves all doctrinal content. Key theological claims are faithfully rendered: (1) Peter's prohibition against burdening consciences with rites ('lelkiismeretet sok szertartással terheljék') matches the source; (2) Paul's condemnation of food prohibitions as 'doctrine of devils' is rendered as 'ördögi tanításnak nevezi,' consistent with the glossary term 'Teufelslehre -> ördögi tanítás'; (3) The crucial doctrinal distinction that bodily discipline must not be done 'to merit grace or make satisfaction for sins' is preserved as 'nem azért, hogy ilyen gyakorlatok által kegyelmet érdemeljünk vagy elégtételt tegyünk a bűnökért'; (4) The definition of true mortification as 'to be crucified with Christ' is rendered as 'Krisztussal megfeszíttetni'; (5) The anti-works-righteousness thrust of the entire passage is fully maintained. All Scripture references (Luke 21:34, Matt. 17:21, 1 Cor. 9:27) are correctly cited and translated. The condemnation of earning grace through rites and the defense against the Jovinian accusation are both fully intact.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All key theological terms align with the approved glossary: 'Speiseverbote -> ételek tiltása' (rendered as 'ételek tiltását'); 'Teufelslehre -> ördögi tanítás' (rendered as 'ördögi tanításnak'); 'Fleisch abtöten / Mortifikation -> a test megöldöklése' (rendered as 'a test megöldöklésének' and 'megöldöklése a testnek'); 'mit Christus gekreuzigt werden -> Krisztussal megfeszíttetni' (rendered exactly); 'leibliche Übungen / äußerliche Zucht -> testi gyakorlatok / külső fegyelmezés' (rendered as 'testi gyakorlatok' and 'külső fegyelmezést'); 'Gewissen -> lelkiismeret' (rendered as 'lelkiismeretet'); 'Genugtuung für Sünden -> elégtétel a bűnökért' (rendered exactly); 'Gnade verdienen -> kegyelmet érdemelni' (rendered as 'kegyelmet érdemeljünk'); 'Evangelium -> evangélium' (rendered as 'evangélium'). 'Disziplin -> fegyelmezés' is used consistently. All terms are standard and consistent throughout.

clarity.explanation: The translation is generally very clear. The argument flows logically and each paragraph is easy to follow. One slightly awkward construction appears: 'Ez az igazi, komoly és nem tettetett megöldöklése a testnek, tudniillik különféle szenvedésekkel gyakoroltatni és Krisztussal megfeszíttetni' — the passive infinitive constructions ('gyakoroltatni,' 'megfeszíttetni') require a moment's pause but are grammatically acceptable in formal theological Hungarian and do not obscure meaning. The rest of the text reads smoothly, and the Scripture quotations are clear and recognizable to a Hungarian reader.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads in a formal, elevated register appropriate for confessional theological prose. Most sentences flow naturally: 'Vigyázzatok, hogy a ti szívetek meg ne nehezedjék dobzódással' and 'Megsanyargatom testemet és szolgává teszem' are natural Hungarian renderings of the Scripture verses. The phrase 'megöldöklése a testnek, tudniillik különféle szenvedésekkel gyakoroltatni' is somewhat archaic and slightly inverted in word order, but this is characteristic of formal confessional style and not problematic. The phrase 'ilyen istentisztelet nélkül' (for 'without such service of God') is natural. Overall the text would be recognizable and acceptable to Hungarian Lutheran readers familiar with confessional documents.

suggestions: The source phrase 'without Buch service of God' appears to be a typo in the source text (likely 'such service of God'); the translation correctly renders it as 'ilyen istentisztelet nélkül,' which is the right interpretation.

The note 'Buch service of God' in the source text appears to be a typo for 'such service of God' — the translation correctly renders it as 'ilyen istentisztelet nélkül,' which is accurate and appropriate.
Consider whether 'nem tettetett' (for 'unfeigned') might be rendered as 'nem képmutató' or 'őszinte' in some contexts, though 'nem tettetett' is acceptable in formal theological Hungarian.

The phrase 'megöldöklése a testnek, tudniillik különféle szenvedésekkel gyakoroltatni és Krisztussal megfeszíttetni' could be slightly restructured for greater clarity: 'tudniillik az, hogy különféle szenvedésekkel próbáltatunk és Krisztussal megfeszíttetünk' — but this is a minor stylistic preference, not a doctrinal concern.

The phrase 'különféle szenvedésekkel gyakoroltatni és Krisztussal megfeszíttetni' uses passive infinitives in series that, while doctrinally correct, could be rendered slightly more naturally as 'hogy különféle szenvedésekben edződjünk és Krisztussal megfeszíttessünk' — though this is a stylistic preference, not a doctrinal concern.

Consider whether 'fegyelmezés' (for 'discipline') is the most natural Hungarian term in context, or whether 'önfegyelem' might be clearer to a modern reader, though 'fegyelmezés' is consistent with the glossary entry 'Disziplin -> fegyelmezés'.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Hier verbietet Petrus, das Gewissen mit vielen Zeremonien zu beschweren, sei es mit mosaischen oder anderen. Und in 1Tim. 4, 1.3 nennt Paulus das Verbot von Speisen eine teuflische Lehre; denn es ist gegen das Evangelium, solche Werke anzuordnen oder zu vollbringen, durch die wir Gnade verdienen, oder als ob das Christentum ohne solchen Gottesdienst nicht bestehen könnte.

Hier werfen uns unsere Feinde vor, dass unsere Lehrer Feinde der Zucht und der Abtötung des Fleisches sind, wie Jovinian. Aber das Gegenteil können wir aus den Schriften unserer Lehrer lernen. Denn sie haben immer so vom Kreuz gelehrt, dass die Christen Leiden tragen müssen. Dies ist die wahre, ernsthafte und nicht erheuchelte Abtötung des Fleisches, nämlich durch mancherlei Leiden geübt und mit Christus gekreuzigt zu werden.

Ferner lehren sie, dass jeder Christ sich durch leibliche Übungen und Mühen in Zucht halten und zügeln muss, damit ihn weder Sattheit noch Trägheit zur Sünde verführe, aber nicht deshalb, damit wir durch solche Übungen Gnade verdienen oder Genugtuung für die Sünden leisten. Und solche äußerliche Zucht muss allezeit geübt werden, nicht nur an einigen bestimmten Tagen. So gebietet Christus, Lk. 21, 34: Sehet aber auf euch selbst, dass eure Herzen nicht beschwert werden mit Fressen; ferner Mt. 17, 21: Diese Art aber geht nicht aus, denn durch Beten und Fasten. Auch Paulus sagt, 1Kor. 9, 27: Ich zähme meinen Leib und halte ihn in Knechtschaft.

differences.original_phrase: a doctrine of devils
differences.back_translated_phrase: eine teuflische Lehre
differences.explanation: The original 'doctrine of devils' (referencing 1 Tim. 4:1's 'didaskaliais daimoniōn') is rendered as 'a devilish/diabolical doctrine.' The back-translation slightly softens the direct Pauline allusion to demonic origin, but the doctrinal meaning remains essentially the same.

differences.original_phrase: without Buch service of God
differences.back_translated_phrase: ohne solchen Gottesdienst
differences.explanation: The original has a likely typo ('Buch' instead of 'such'). The back-translation renders this as 'without such worship/service of God,' which correctly interprets the intended meaning. No doctrinal loss.

differences.original_phrase: our adversaries
differences.back_translated_phrase: unsere Feinde
differences.explanation: 'Adversaries' is rendered as 'enemies' (Feinde). This is a slightly stronger term but does not change the doctrinal meaning in context.

differences.original_phrase: train and subdue himself with bodily restraints, or bodily exercises and labors
differences.back_translated_phrase: sich durch leibliche Übungen und Mühen in Zucht halten und zügeln
differences.explanation: The phrase 'bodily restraints' from the original is not explicitly rendered; the back-translation uses only 'bodily exercises and labors.' The meaning is slightly condensed but the doctrinal point about physical discipline remains intact.

differences.original_phrase: Take heed lest your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting
differences.back_translated_phrase: Sehet aber auf euch selbst, dass eure Herzen nicht beschwert werden mit Fressen
differences.explanation: 'Surfeiting' is rendered as 'Fressen' (gorging/gluttony), which is a more colloquial term but captures the same meaning. No doctrinal difference.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is well preserved throughout the back-translation. The key theological points — that rites and food prohibitions must not be used to merit grace, that true mortification involves bearing afflictions with Christ, and that bodily discipline is required at all times rather than on set days alone — are all faithfully rendered. Differences are minor and stylistic, with no doctrinal distortion detected.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation preserves all doctrinal content faithfully. Key theological distinctions are maintained: (1) The purpose of bodily discipline ('not to merit forgiveness of sins') is correctly rendered as 'nem pedig azért, hogy ezzel a fegyelmezéssel érdemet szerezzen, bűnbocsánatot' — the anti-merit doctrine is intact. (2) 'Necessary service' (notwendiger Gottesdienst) is rendered 'szükséges istentisztelet,' correctly preserved as 'szükséges istentisztelet volnának.' (3) The distinction between condemning fasting itself vs. traditions that prescribe fasting with peril of conscience is clearly preserved. (4) The statement that observances 'do not justify before God' is accurately rendered 'nem szereznek Isten előtti megigazulást.' (5) The Irenaeus quote about diversity in fasting not destroying the harmony/unity of faith is faithfully translated. (6) Pope Gregory's reference and the point about church unity are correctly preserved. No doctrinal content has been added, lost, or distorted.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All glossary-relevant terms are used correctly and consistently: 'bűnbocsánat' (Vergebung der Sünden), 'istentisztelet' (Gottesdienst/necessary service), 'lelkiismeret' (Gewissen), 'egyházatyák' (Fathers/Väter), 'emberi hagyományok' (menschliche Traditionen), 'szertartások' (Riten/Observanzen), 'megigazulás' context properly rendered as 'Isten előtti megigazulást' (Rechtfertigung vor Gott), 'egyház egysége' (Einheit der Kirche), 'szakadás' (Schisma), 'hivatás' (Beruf), 'böjt' (Fasten), 'húsvét' (Ostern). The term 'hit egységét' for 'harmony of faith' is a reasonable rendering — the source says 'harmoniam fidei' which can be rendered as unity/harmony of faith. All approved glossary terms that appear in this passage are used consistently.

clarity.explanation: The translation is clear and easily understandable throughout. The argument flows logically: the purpose of bodily discipline → condemnation of traditions (not fasting itself) → observance of church orders with the caveat they don't justify → historical example of Easter dating diversity → Irenaeus and Gregory citations. One potentially complex sentence ('nem szabad bűnné tenni, ha azokat botránkozás nélkül elmulasztják') is rendered clearly. The Dist. XII reference is preserved as-is, which is appropriate for a technical citation. No passages require re-reading to understand.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads naturally for theological prose in Hungarian. The elevated, formal register is appropriate for confessional text. One minor awkwardness: 'nem fegyelmezi a testét, nem pedig azért' — the opening 'azért fegyelmezi a testét, nem pedig azért' has a slight redundancy in the 'azért...nem pedig azért' construction that a native writer might phrase slightly differently, though it remains comprehensible. The sentence 'Irenaeus mondja: A böjtöt illető különbözőség nem rontja le a hit egységét' reads naturally. Overall the text sounds like appropriate Hungarian theological writing.

suggestions: The phrase 'nem szereznek Isten előtti megigazulást' is technically accurate but could also be rendered 'nem igazítanak meg Isten előtt' to more closely follow the glossary's active verb form 'megigazít' (rechtfertigt) — again a minor stylistic note with no doctrinal impact.

The opening sentence 'azért fegyelmezi a testét, nem pedig azért, hogy ezzel a fegyelmezéssel érdemet szerezzen' could be smoothed slightly to avoid the repeated 'azért' construction, e.g., 'azért tartja testét fegyelem alatt, nem azért, hogy ezzel a fegyelmezéssel érdemet szerezzen' — though this is a style preference, not a doctrinal concern.
No critical theological or terminological changes are needed; the translation is accurate and reliable for pastoral and congregational use.
The phrase 'Irenaeus mondja' renders 'Irenaeus says' but the source has 'Irenaeus Bays' (clearly a typo for 'says') — the translation correctly interprets this as 'mondja', which is appropriate.

In the opening sentence, consider restructuring slightly for smoother flow: 'azért fegyelmezi a testét, nem azért, hogy ezzel a fegyelmezéssel bűnbocsánatot érdemeljen' — placing 'bűnbocsánatot' before the verb 'érdemeljen' would feel more natural in Hungarian.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Hier zeigt er klar, dass er deshalb den Leib kasteit, nicht aber deshalb, um durch diese Kasteiung Verdienst zu erwerben, Sündenvergebung, sondern um den Leib im Gehorsam zu halten und tauglich zu sein für die geistlichen Dinge sowie für die Erfüllung der Pflicht gemäß seiner Berufung. Daher verdammen wir nicht das Fasten selbst, sondern jene menschlichen Überlieferungen, welche bestimmte Tage und bestimmte Speisen vorschreiben unter Gefährdung des Gewissens, als ob solche Werke ein notwendiger Gottesdienst wären.

Gleichwohl werden bei uns sehr viele Überlieferungen gehalten, die zur guten Ordnung in der Kirche gehören, wie die Ordnung der Lesungen in der Messe und die hohen Festtage. Aber zugleich werden die Menschen darauf hingewiesen, dass solche Zeremonien keine Rechtfertigung vor Gott erwerben, und dass man in solchen Dingen keine Sünde daraus machen soll, wenn man sie ohne Ärgernis weglässt. Eine solche Freiheit in menschlichen Zeremonien war den Kirchenvätern nicht unbekannt. Denn im Osten hielt man Ostern zu einer anderen Zeit als in Rom, und als die Römer die östliche Kirche wegen dieser Verschiedenheit der Spaltung beschuldigten, wiesen andere sie darauf hin, dass solche Gebräuche nicht überall gleich sein müssen. Und Irenäus sagt: Die Verschiedenheit hinsichtlich des Fastens hebt die Einheit des Glaubens nicht auf; wie auch Papst Gregor in Dist. XII anzeigt, dass eine solche Verschiedenheit die Einheit der Kirche nicht verletzt.

differences.original_phrase: not to merit, forgiveness of sins by that discipline
differences.back_translated_phrase: nicht aber deshalb, um durch diese Kasteiung Verdienst zu erwerben, Sündenvergebung
differences.explanation: The back-translation renders this somewhat awkwardly ('Verdienst zu erwerben, Sündenvergebung') but the doctrinal meaning — that discipline is not done to merit forgiveness of sins — is preserved.

differences.original_phrase: the traditions which prescribe certain days and certain meats
differences.back_translated_phrase: jene menschlichen Überlieferungen, welche bestimmte Tage und bestimmte Speisen vorschreiben
differences.explanation: The back-translation adds 'menschlichen' (human) before 'Überlieferungen' (traditions), which is a slight addition but not doctrinally significant in context.

differences.original_phrase: such works were a necessary service
differences.back_translated_phrase: solche Werke ein notwendiger Gottesdienst wären
differences.explanation: 'Gottesdienst' (divine service/worship) is more specific than the generic 'service' in the original, but the meaning of works being treated as necessary religious observance is preserved.

differences.original_phrase: which conduce to good order in the Church
differences.back_translated_phrase: die zur guten Ordnung in der Kirche gehören
differences.explanation: 'gehören' (belong to) slightly differs from 'conduce to' (contribute to), but the overall sense of traditions maintaining church order is preserved.

differences.original_phrase: such observances do not justify before God
differences.back_translated_phrase: solche Zeremonien keine Rechtfertigung vor Gott erwerben
differences.explanation: The back-translation uses 'Zeremonien' (ceremonies) instead of 'observances,' and 'erwerben' (earn/acquire) adds a nuance of merit-seeking, but the core doctrinal point that such practices do not justify before God is intact.

differences.original_phrase: in such things it should not be made sin if they be omitted without offense
differences.back_translated_phrase: dass man in solchen Dingen keine Sünde daraus machen soll, wenn man sie ohne Ärgernis weglässt
differences.explanation: The meaning is well preserved. 'Ärgernis' (offense/scandal) accurately reflects the original intent.

differences.original_phrase: Such liberty in human rites was not unknown to the Fathers
differences.back_translated_phrase: Eine solche Freiheit in menschlichen Zeremonien war den Kirchenvätern nicht unbekannt
differences.explanation: 'Zeremonien' (ceremonies) is used instead of 'rites,' and 'Kirchenvätern' (Church Fathers) is more specific than 'the Fathers,' but the meaning is preserved.

differences.original_phrase: Irenaeus says
differences.back_translated_phrase: Irenäus sagt
differences.explanation: The original has 'Irenaeus Bays' (likely a typo for 'says'), and the back-translation correctly renders this as 'says.' No meaningful difference.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is well preserved across both texts. All differences are minor stylistic or lexical variations that do not alter the theological content. The key Lutheran doctrinal points — that fasting is not meritorious, that human traditions do not justify, that omitting such observances is not sin, and that diversity in rites does not destroy church unity — are faithfully represented in the back-translation.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation accurately preserves all doctrinal content. 'It was not the mind of the Apostles to enact rules concerning holy-days' is correctly rendered as 'Nem az volt az apostolok szándéka, hogy ünnepnapokra vonatkozó rendeléseket hozzanak' — the key theological distinction between apostolic authority and human ordinances is intact. 'To preach godliness and a holy life' is rendered as 'kegyességet és szent életet prédikáljanak,' accurately preserving the apostolic mission. The bracketed addition '[, hogy hitet és szeretetet tanítsanak]' correctly mirrors the bracketed '[, to teach faith and love]' in the source. The reference to the Tripartite History, Book 9, is accurately preserved. No doctrinal content has been added, removed, or distorted.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All glossary terms used in this passage are applied consistently: 'Tripartite History' → 'Háromrészes Egyháztörténet' (matches glossary), 'Apostles' → 'apostolok' (matches glossary), 'holy-days' → 'ünnepnapok' (matches glossary), 'Gottseligkeit/godliness' → 'kegyességet' (matches 'Gottseligkeit -> kegyesség' in glossary), 'heiliges Leben/holy life' → 'szent életet' (matches 'heiliges Leben -> szent élet'), 'Liebe/love' → 'szeretetet' (matches 'Liebe -> szeretet'), 'Glaube/faith' → 'hitet' (matches 'Glaube -> hit'). The term 'rendeléseket' for 'rules/ordinances' is consistent with the established pattern of 'Menschensatzungen -> emberi rendelések' throughout the glossary.

clarity.explanation: The Hungarian translation is clear and immediately understandable. The sentence structure follows natural Hungarian syntax. The source attribution ('a Háromrészes Egyháztörténetben, a 9. könyvben'), the summarizing statement ('a következő kijelentés található'), and the quoted content are all logically organized. The bracketed portion '[, hogy hitet és szeretetet tanítsanak]' is visually and grammatically integrated in the same way as the source's bracket. No passage requires re-reading.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads as natural Hungarian theological prose. Constructions like 'számos példa van összegyűjtve' (many examples are gathered) and 'a következő kijelentés található' (the following statement is found) are idiomatic. The infinitive constructions 'rendeléseket hozzanak,' 'kegyességet és szent életet prédikáljanak,' and 'hitet és szeretetet tanítsanak' are grammatically correct and natural in Hungarian. The elevated register is appropriate for confessional theological writing.

suggestions: No corrections needed. The translation is accurate, consistent, clear, and natural. It faithfully represents both the doctrinal content and the literary structure of the source text, including the bracketed addendum.
No changes are necessary. The translation is accurate, consistent, clear, and natural.

back_translation.back_translated_text: Und in der dreiteiligen Kirchengeschichte, im 9. Buch, sind zahlreiche Beispiele über abweichende Zeremonien gesammelt, und folgende Aussage findet sich dort: Es war nicht die Absicht der Apostel, Anordnungen bezüglich Festtagen zu erlassen, sondern Frömmigkeit und heiliges Leben zu predigen [, Glauben und Liebe zu lehren].

differences.original_phrase: the Tripartite History
differences.back_translated_phrase: der dreiteiligen Kirchengeschichte
differences.explanation: The back-translation renders 'Tripartite History' as 'dreiteiligen Kirchengeschichte' (Tripartite Church History), adding the word 'Kirchengeschichte' (Church History). This is actually the full, correct title of the work (Historia Ecclesiastica Tripartita), so the back-translation is arguably more precise. No doctrinal meaning is changed.

differences.original_phrase: dissimilar rites
differences.back_translated_phrase: abweichende Zeremonien
differences.explanation: 'Dissimilar rites' becomes 'abweichende Zeremonien' (divergent/deviating ceremonies). The nuance shifts slightly from 'different from each other' to 'diverging from a norm,' but this is a minor stylistic difference with no doctrinal impact.

differences.original_phrase: to enact rules concerning holy-days
differences.back_translated_phrase: Anordnungen bezüglich Festtagen zu erlassen
differences.explanation: 'Rules' becomes 'Anordnungen' (ordinances/orders/directives). The meaning is essentially the same — legislating about festival days — with no doctrinal change.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is fully preserved. The back-translation accurately captures the argument that the Apostles' intent was not to legislate church ceremonies but to preach godliness, faith, and love. The minor differences are stylistic or represent slight elaborations that do not alter theological content.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation accurately preserves all doctrinal content of this passage. Key theological claims are faithfully rendered: (1) The historical account of monasteries being 'free associations' in Augustine's time ('szabad társulások') is correct. (2) The addition of vows to restore discipline, likened to 'a carefully planned prison' ('gondosan megtervezett börtönként'), is accurate. (3) The condemnation of vows taken before canonical age ('a kánoni kor előtt') is preserved. (4) The statement about people being ensnared and compelled to remain ('tőrbe csalva arra kényszerültek, hogy ott maradjanak') is doctrinally precise. (5) The reference to canonical provision for release ('a kánonok kegyes rendelkezése révén fel lehetett volna szabadítani') is accurate. (6) The concern about women in convents and the 'weaker sex' ('gyengébb nem') is preserved. (7) The condemnation of throwing young men and maidens into convents for a living ('megélhetés végett kolostorokba vetettek') is accurate. (8) The triple concern about scandals, snares for consciences, and disregard for canonical authority is fully preserved ('botránkozások', 'tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretnek', 'kánoni tekintélyt... teljesen félretették és megvetették').

terminology_consistency.explanation: All key terms from the approved glossary are used consistently: 'Kanones' -> 'kánonok' (used correctly multiple times: 'a kánonok ellenére', 'a kánoni kor előtt', 'a kánonok kegyes rendelkezése', 'kánoni tekintélyt'); 'Klöster' -> 'kolostorok' (correct throughout); 'Nonnenklöster' -> 'apácakolostorok' (correct); 'Ärgernisse' -> 'botránkozások' (correct); 'Stricke für die Gewissen' -> 'tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretnek' (consistent with glossary 'a lelkiismeret számára vetett tőrök'); 'Mönchsgelübde' -> 'szerzetesi fogadalmakról' (consistent with glossary); 'kanonisches Alter' -> 'kánoni kor' (consistent); 'Ansehen der Kanones' -> 'kánoni tekintélyt' (consistent). The term 'Disziplin' is translated as 'fegyelmezés' which aligns with the glossary entry. One minor note: 'kolositorokban' appears to be a typo for 'kolostorokban' but this is a typographical error, not a terminological inconsistency.

clarity.explanation: The translation is generally clear and readable. Most sentences communicate their meaning effectively on first reading. One minor issue: 'tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretnek' (snares were cast upon consciences) is a somewhat archaic construction in Hungarian but is understandable in theological context. The phrase 'milyen tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretnek' follows the source closely and may require a moment's reflection for a modern Hungarian reader. The exclamatory structure of the final rhetorical passage ('Látták... Bánkódtak...') is well-rendered and clear. There is also a typographical error 'kolositorokban' instead of 'kolostorokban' which slightly disrupts reading flow.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads naturally for a formal theological/confessional register appropriate to this type of document. Constructions like 'mennyi minden történt napról napra' (how many things were done day by day) are idiomatic Hungarian. 'Megélhetés végett kolostorokba vetettek' reads naturally. 'Így tőrbe csalva arra kényszerültek, hogy ott maradjanak' is slightly formal but appropriate. The minor typographical error 'kolositorokban' (should be 'kolostorokban') is the only notable flaw that would catch a native reader's attention. Overall the text reads as competent theological Hungarian prose with appropriate elevated register.

suggestions: Correct the typographical error 'kolositorokban' to 'kolostorokban' in the first paragraph.

The phrase 'Ez a szigor sok jó embert bántott már korábban is' could be rendered more precisely as 'Ez a szigor már korábban is sok jó embert szomorított/bántott' — the current version is acceptable but 'bántott' is slightly colloquial for a confessional text; 'szomorított' or 'aggasztott' might better fit the elevated register.
Consider revising 'milyen tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretnek' to 'milyen tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretre' for more natural Hungarian preposition use.

The phrase 'Ez a szigor sok jó embert bántott már korábban is' — while understandable — could be rendered more precisely as 'Ez a szigorúság már korábban is sokakban felháborodást keltett' to better capture the sense of 'displeased many good men before this time,' though the current rendering is doctrinally acceptable.
Correct the typographical error 'kolositorokban' to 'kolostorokba(n)' in the first paragraph.

Consider rendering 'milyen tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeretnek' as 'milyen tőröket vetettek a lelkiismeret elé' or 'milyen hurkokat vetettek a lelkiismeretre' for slightly more natural Hungarian idiomatic expression, though the current rendering is doctrinally acceptable.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
XXVII. Artikel: Von den Mönchsgelübden.

Was wir auf unserer Seite von den Mönchsgelübden lehren, wird besser verständlich sein, wenn man sich daran erinnert, in welchem Zustand die Klöster waren, und wie vieles täglich gerade in jenen Klöstern gegen die Kanones geschah. Zu Augustins Zeiten waren diese freie Vereinigungen. Später, als die Disziplin verfiel, wurden überall Gelübde eingeführt, um die Disziplin wiederherzustellen, gleichsam als sorgfältig geplantes Gefängnis.

Nach und nach wurden neben den Gelübden viele weitere Zeremonien eingeführt. Und diese Fesseln wurden vielen vor dem kanonischen Alter auferlegt, gegen die Kanones.

Viele traten auch aus Unwissenheit in diese Lebensform ein, weil sie nicht in der Lage waren, ihre eigene Kraft einzuschätzen, obwohl sie alt genug waren. So in eine Falle gelockt, wurden sie gezwungen, dort zu bleiben, obwohl einige durch die gnädige Bestimmung der Kanones hätten befreit werden können. Und dies war eher bei den Nonnenklöstern so als bei den der Mönche, obwohl dem schwächeren Geschlecht gegenüber mehr Aufmerksamkeit hätte gezeigt werden müssen. Diese Strenge hatte viele gute Menschen schon früher betrübt, die sahen, wie Jünglinge und Jungfrauen des Unterhalts wegen in Klöster geworfen wurden. Sie sahen, welche unglücklichen Folgen diese Vorgehensweise hatte, und welche Ärgernisse entstanden, welche Schlingen dem Gewissen gelegt wurden. Sie beklagten, dass die kanonische Autorität in einer so schwerwiegenden Sache völlig beiseitegelegt und verachtet wurde.

differences.original_phrase: many other observances were added besides vows
differences.back_translated_phrase: viele weitere Zeremonien eingeführt
differences.explanation: 'Observances' (Praktiken/Bräuche) is rendered as 'Zeremonien' (ceremonies). While similar, 'observances' is broader and includes rules and practices beyond formal ceremonies. The doctrinal point remains the same.

differences.original_phrase: though some could have been freed by the kind provision of the Canons
differences.back_translated_phrase: obwohl einige durch die gnädige Bestimmung der Kanones hätten befreit werden können
differences.explanation: The back-translation uses 'gnädige' (gracious/merciful) for 'kind,' which is a close synonym. No doctrinal difference.

differences.original_phrase: more consideration should have been shown the weaker sex
differences.back_translated_phrase: mehr Aufmerksamkeit hätte gezeigt werden müssen
differences.explanation: 'Consideration' is rendered as 'Aufmerksamkeit' (attention) rather than something like 'Rücksicht' (consideration/regard). The meaning is functionally preserved in context.

differences.original_phrase: for a living
differences.back_translated_phrase: des Unterhalts wegen
differences.explanation: The back-translation renders this as 'for the sake of sustenance/livelihood,' which accurately captures the original meaning that young people were placed in monasteries for economic reasons.

differences.original_phrase: what scandals were created
differences.back_translated_phrase: welche Ärgernisse entstanden
differences.explanation: 'Scandals' and 'Ärgernisse' (offenses/scandals) are essentially equivalent. No meaningful difference.

differences.original_phrase: They were grieved that the authority of the Canons in so momentous a matter was utterly set aside and despised
differences.back_translated_phrase: Sie beklagten, dass die kanonische Autorität in einer so schwerwiegenden Sache völlig beiseitegelegt und verachtet wurde
differences.explanation: 'They were grieved' becomes 'Sie beklagten' (they lamented/complained), which shifts slightly from an emotional state (grief) to an active expression (complaint), but the overall sentiment of disapproval is preserved.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is fully preserved across both texts. The back-translation accurately conveys the Confessional critique of monastic vows: their historical corruption, imposition on those below canonical age, entrapment through ignorance, harsher treatment of women in convents, and the setting aside of canonical protections. All differences are minor stylistic or lexical variations with no theological consequence.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation preserves all doctrinal content faithfully. Key theological claims are accurately rendered: 'vows were equal to Baptism' → 'a fogadalmak egyenlők a keresztséggel'; 'merited forgiveness of sins and justification before God' → 'bűnbocsánatot és Isten előtti megigazulást érdemelnek'; 'evangelical counsels' → 'evangéliumi tanácsokat'; 'the profession of monasticism was far better than Baptism' → 'a szerzetesi fogadalom letétele sokkal jobb a keresztségnél'; 'state of perfection' → 'tökéletesség állapota'. The bracket notation for the interpolated sentence '[Moreover, a person who has been thus ensnared...]' is correctly preserved as '[Továbbá az, akit így behálóztak és kolostorba lépett, keveset tanul Krisztusról.]'. All condemnations, comparisons, and doctrinal distinctions between monastic life, baptism, and God-ordained callings are intact.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All glossary terms are used correctly and consistently: 'Baptism' → 'keresztség' (consistent); 'justification before God' / 'Rechtfertigung vor Gott' → 'Isten előtti megigazulás' / 'Isten előtt megigazulást' (consistent); 'evangelical counsels' / 'evangelische Räte' → 'evangéliumi tanácsokat' (consistent); 'state of perfection' / 'Stand der Vollkommenheit' → 'tökéletesség állapota' (consistent); 'pastors' / 'Pastoren' → 'lelkészek' (consistent); 'monastic life' / 'mönchisches Leben' → 'szerzetesi élet' (consistent); 'monasteries' / 'Klöster' → 'kolostorokban' (consistent); 'vows' / 'Gelübde' → 'fogadalmak' (consistent); 'forgiveness of sins' / 'Vergebung der Sünden' → 'bűnbocsánatot' (consistent); 'civil magistrates' / 'weltliche Obrigkeit' → 'világi hatóságé' (consistent). No inconsistencies detected.

clarity.explanation: The translation is clear and readily understandable throughout. The argument flows logically from paragraph to paragraph. Sentences like 'Ezekhez a bajokhoz járult még a fogadalmakról szóló olyan meggyőződés' and 'Mi történt tehát a kolostorokban?' are immediately clear. The rhetorical structure of the original (presenting the problem, giving evidence, then drawing conclusions) is preserved clearly. No passages require re-reading for comprehension. Note: there appears to be a Greek letter 'ν' (nu) in 'kolostorokbaν' instead of a standard 'n', which is a minor typographical issue but does not impede clarity.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads naturally for a formal theological text in Hungarian. Phrases like 'Fölösleges elmondani, amit mindenki ismer' and 'Most más a helyzet' are idiomatic and natural. One minor issue: 'kolostorokbaν' contains what appears to be a Unicode character error (Greek nu instead of Latin n), which is a typographical artifact rather than a translation choice. Slightly formal constructions like 'Ezekhez a bajokhoz járult még' are appropriate for the elevated register of confessional literature. Overall the text reads as natural Hungarian theological prose.

suggestions: Correct the apparent Unicode/typographical error in 'kolostorokbaν' — the 'ν' appears to be a Greek nu character rather than a Latin 'n'; it should read 'kolostorokba'.

Consider whether 'emberi istentisztelet' for 'man-made services' might be clearer as 'emberi rendelések' or 'emberek által kitalált istentisztelet' to align more closely with the glossary entries for 'Menschensatzungen/Menschendienst'.

The phrase 'mindenféle emberi istentisztelet nélkül' for 'without any man-made services' is acceptable but could also be rendered 'mindenféle emberi szerzetesi szolgálat nélkül' to more precisely capture the monastic context, though the current rendering is doctrinally sound.
Correct the typographical error 'kolostorokbaν' (with Greek nu) to 'kolostorokban' in the third paragraph.

Consider whether 'igazságot érdemel Isten előtt' (merited righteousness before God) vs. 'megigazulást érdemel Isten előtt' more precisely captures 'merited righteousness before God' — the glossary consistently uses 'megigazulás' for Rechtfertigung, but 'igazság' is used here in the sense of Gerechtigkeit, which is acceptable in context.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Zu diesen Missständen kam noch die Überzeugung hinsichtlich der Gelübde hinzu, die – wie allgemein bekannt – früher sogar den besonneneren Mönchen nicht gefiel. Man lehrte, dass die Gelübde der Taufe gleichwertig seien; man lehrte, dass man durch eine solche Lebensweise Sündenvergebung und Rechtfertigung vor Gott verdiene. Ja, man fügte hinzu, dass das Mönchsleben nicht nur Gerechtigkeit vor Gott verdiene, sondern noch größere Dinge, weil es nicht nur die Gebote halte, sondern auch die sogenannten „evangelischen Räte".

So überredete man die Menschen, dass die Ablegung des Mönchsgelübdes weit besser sei als die Taufe, und dass das Mönchsleben verdienstvoller sei als das der weltlichen Obrigkeit, als das der Pfarrer und anderer ähnlicher, die ihren Beruf gemäß den Geboten Gottes erfüllen, ohne jeglichen menschlichen Gottesdienst. Von diesen Dingen kann nichts geleugnet werden, denn sie finden sich in ihren eigenen Büchern. [Ferner lernt derjenige, der so verstrickt wurde und in ein Kloster eingetreten ist, wenig über Christus.]

Was geschah also in den Klöstern? Einst waren sie Schulen der Theologie und anderer Wissenschaftszweige, die für die Kirche nützlich waren; und von dort gewann man Pfarrer und Bischöfe. Jetzt ist die Lage anders. Es ist überflüssig zu sagen, was jedermann bekannt ist. Einst kamen sie zusammen, um zu lernen; jetzt täuschen sie vor, dass dies eine Lebensform sei, die zur Verdienung von Gnade und Gerechtigkeit eingesetzt wurde; ja, sie verkündigen, dass dies der Stand der Vollkommenheit sei, und erheben ihn weit über alle anderen von Gott eingesetzten Lebensweisen.

differences.original_phrase: the profession of monasticism
differences.back_translated_phrase: die Ablegung des Mönchsgelübdes
differences.explanation: The original 'profession of monasticism' refers to the formal act of taking monastic vows and entering the life. The back-translation renders this as 'the taking of monastic vows' (Ablegung des Mönchsgelübdes), which is slightly narrower in focus but captures essentially the same act. No doctrinal shift occurs.

differences.original_phrase: than that of magistrates
differences.back_translated_phrase: als das der weltlichen Obrigkeit
differences.explanation: 'Magistrates' is rendered as 'weltliche Obrigkeit' (secular/civil authority), which is a reasonable equivalent in German context. The meaning — that secular governing roles are legitimate God-ordained callings — is preserved.

differences.original_phrase: without any man-made services
differences.back_translated_phrase: ohne jeglichen menschlichen Gottesdienst
differences.explanation: The original 'man-made services' refers to humanly devised religious works/rites. The back-translation uses 'menschlichen Gottesdienst' (human worship/divine service), which is a close and theologically equivalent rendering. No meaningful doctrinal shift.

differences.original_phrase: other branches
differences.back_translated_phrase: anderer Wissenschaftszweige
differences.explanation: 'Other branches' (of learning/study) is rendered as 'other branches of science/scholarship' (Wissenschaftszweige). This is slightly more specific but does not alter the meaning that monasteries served broad educational purposes. No doctrinal impact.

differences.original_phrase: a kind of life instituted to merit grace and righteousness
differences.back_translated_phrase: eine Lebensform, die zur Verdienung von Gnade und Gerechtigkeit eingesetzt wurde
differences.explanation: The back-translation closely mirrors the original. 'Verdienung' (meriting/earning) accurately reflects the theological critique being made. No meaningful change.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is fully preserved throughout the back-translation. All critical theological claims — that monastic vows were falsely equated with Baptism, that monastic life was wrongly taught to merit forgiveness and justification, that monasticism was elevated above God-ordained callings, and that monasteries had strayed from their original educational purpose — are accurately conveyed. The minor differences are purely matters of word choice or slight narrowing of scope, none of which introduce any theological error or doctrinal shift.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation preserves all doctrinal content with high fidelity. Key doctrinal claims are accurately rendered: 'vows cannot annul the ordinance and commandment of God' → 'a fogadalmak nem érvényteleníthetik Isten rendelését és parancsát' is exact. The argument from 1 Cor. 7:2 and Gen. 2:18 is faithfully reproduced, including the distinction between commandment, creation, and ordinance. The canonical principle 'the right of the superior is excepted in every vow' → 'a felsőbbség joga minden fogadalomban kivételt képez' is accurate. The concluding argument about papal dispensation proving vows are not simply divine obligations is correctly rendered: 'nem szabad az embernek olyan kötelezettséget érvénytelenítenie, amely egyszerűen isteni kötelezettség.' The bracketed insertion '[that vows are not binding against the decision of the Pope]' is correctly preserved as a bracketed clause. No doctrinal content has been added, removed, or distorted.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All glossary-relevant terms are used consistently and correctly. 'Ordinance of God' → 'Isten rendelése' (consistent with glossary 'Ordnung Gottes / Gottesordnung → Isten rendelése'). 'Vows' → 'fogadalmak' (consistent with glossary 'Gelübde → fogadalmak'). 'Canons' → 'egyházi jog' (the source says 'The Canons teach' and the translation uses 'Az egyházjog azt tanítja', which maps correctly to 'Kanonisches Recht / Kanones → egyházjog / kánonok'). 'Fornication' → 'paráznaság' (consistent with 'Unzucht / Hurerei → paráznaság'). 'Roman Pontiff' → 'római pápa' (consistent with 'Römischer Pontifex / Papst → római főpap / pápa'). 'Dispensation' → 'diszpenzációt' (consistent with 'Dispensation → diszpenzáció'). 'Teachers' → 'tanítóinknak' (consistent with 'Lehrer / unsere Lehrer → tanítók / a mi tanítóink'). 'Divine obligation' → 'isteni kötelezettség' (consistent with 'göttliche Verpflichtung → isteni kötelezettség').

clarity.explanation: The translation is clear and immediately understandable to an educated Hungarian speaker. The argument structure (first establishing the lawfulness of marriage, then addressing objections about vows, then the papal dispensation argument) flows logically. The sentence 'Ezért nem vétkeznek azok, akik Istennek ezen parancsának és rendelésének engedelmeskednek' is clear and direct. The final sentence 'mert nem szabad az embernek olyan kötelezettséget érvénytelenítenie, amely egyszerűen isteni kötelezettség' accurately and clearly conveys the meaning of 'an obligation which is simply divine.' No passages require re-reading.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads naturally in an elevated, confessional register appropriate for this type of document. Biblical quotations are rendered in traditional Hungarian biblical language ('tulajdon felesége legyen,' 'Nem jó az embernek egyedül lenni'), which is fitting. Minor traces of translation influence appear in constructions like 'azok favour, akik a magányos életre nem alkalmasak' and 'arra kényszeríti azokat a házasságra, akiket Isten különleges műve alól ki nem vett,' which are grammatically correct but slightly formal. The phrase 'amennyire csak akarják' ('as much as they list') is a natural Hungarian idiom. Overall registers well as formal theological prose.

suggestions: The Canonical quotation is rendered as 'Az egyházjog azt tanítja' rather than specifying 'A kánonok azt tanítják' — consider whether the more literal rendering of 'Canons' as 'kánonok' would better serve theological precision, since the approved glossary lists 'Canons → egyházi kánonok'.

Consider rendering 'fogadalom kötelező ereje' (per the glossary entry for 'Verbindlichkeit des Gelübdes') instead of 'fogadalom kötelezettségét' for stricter glossary compliance, though the current rendering is doctrinally equivalent.

The phrase 'arra kényszeríti azokat a házasságra' (forces those to marry) could be restructured as 'azokat is arra kényszeríti, hogy házasságra lépjenek' to improve flow, though the current rendering is not incorrect.

The phrase 'amely egyszerűen isteni kötelezettség' for 'which is simply divine' could optionally be rendered more explicitly as 'amely egyszerűen isteni természetű kötelezettség' to avoid ambiguity about whether 'egyszerűen' means 'simply/merely' or 'plainly,' though context makes the intended meaning clear.
Consider rendering 'megértessék' as 'megérthető legyen' or 'jobban megértsék' for slightly improved clarity, though the archaic passive is not incorrect in formal register.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Diese Dinge haben wir ohne gehässige Übertreibung aufgeführt, damit die Lehre unserer Lehrer in diesem Punkt besser verstanden werde.

Erstens, was diejenigen betrifft, die in den Ehestand treten, lehren die Unsrigen, dass es jedem Menschen frei steht, eine Ehe zu schließen, der zum ehelosen Leben nicht geeignet ist, weil die Gelübde die Ordnung und das Gebot Gottes nicht ungültig machen können. Das Gebot Gottes aber ist dieses, 1Kor. 7, 2: Aber um der Hurerei willen habe jeder Mann seine eigene Frau, und jede Frau ihren eigenen Mann. Und nicht nur das Gebot, sondern auch Gottes Schöpfung und Ordnung zwingt diejenigen zur Ehe, die Gott nicht durch sein besonderes Werk ausgenommen hat, gemäß folgendem Wort, 1Mose 2, 18: Und Gott der Herr sprach: Es ist nicht gut, dass der Mensch allein sei. Daher sündigen nicht diejenigen, die diesem Gebot und dieser Ordnung Gottes gehorchen.

Was kann man dagegen einwenden? Die Menschen mögen die Verbindlichkeit des Gelübdes so sehr rühmen, wie sie wollen, sie können es dennoch nicht dahin bringen, dass das Gelübde das Gebot Gottes ungültig macht. Das Kirchenrecht lehrt, dass das Recht der Obrigkeit in jedem Gelübde einen Vorbehalt bildet; [dass die Gelübde gegenüber der Entscheidung des Papstes nicht bindend sind;] umso weniger gelten also jene Gelübde, die gegen die Gebote Gottes sind.

Wenn nun aber die Verbindlichkeit der Gelübde aus keinem Grunde geändert werden könnte, hätte der römische Papst niemals eine Dispens erteilen können; denn es steht dem Menschen nicht frei, eine solche Verbindlichkeit ungültig zu machen, die schlechterdings eine göttliche Verbindlichkeit ist.

differences.original_phrase: let every man have his own wife
differences.back_translated_phrase: Aber um der Hurerei willen habe jeder Mann seine eigene Frau, und jede Frau ihren eigenen Mann
differences.explanation: The back-translation renders the full biblical quotation from 1 Cor. 7:2 more completely, adding 'and every woman her own husband.' This is actually a more accurate rendering of the scripture, not a doctrinal change, and does not alter the argument being made.

differences.original_phrase: according to the text Gen. 2, 18: It is not good that the man should be alone
differences.back_translated_phrase: gemäß folgendem Wort, 1Mose 2, 18: Und Gott der Herr sprach: Es ist nicht gut, dass der Mensch allein sei
differences.explanation: The back-translation adds a narrative introduction ('And God the Lord spoke:') to the Genesis quotation. This is a stylistic expansion of the quote and does not alter the doctrinal meaning.

differences.original_phrase: the right of the superior is excepted in every vow
differences.back_translated_phrase: das Recht der Obrigkeit in jedem Gelübde einen Vorbehalt bildet
differences.explanation: The original says the right of the superior is 'excepted' (i.e., reserved/excluded from the vow's binding force), while the back-translation says it 'forms a reservation.' The meaning is functionally equivalent — the superior's authority supersedes the vow — but the phrasing is slightly different.

differences.original_phrase: for it is not lawful for man to annul an obligation which is simply divine
differences.back_translated_phrase: denn es steht dem Menschen nicht frei, eine solche Verbindlichkeit ungültig zu machen, die schlechterdings eine göttliche Verbindlichkeit ist
differences.explanation: The back-translation adds 'eine solche' (such an) and explicitly restates 'Verbindlichkeit' (obligation) at the end, making the sentence slightly more redundant but preserving the full meaning. No doctrinal change.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is fully preserved across both texts. The back-translation accurately conveys all the key theological arguments: the lawfulness of marriage for those not gifted for celibacy, the primacy of God's commandment over vows, the limited authority of canonical vows, and the argument from papal dispensation practice. The differences are all minor stylistic or expansive renderings of scripture quotations that do not affect the doctrinal content.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation accurately preserves all doctrinal content from the source. Key theological claims are faithfully rendered: (1) The argument about dispensations from vows ('felmentést fogadalmak alól') mirrors the source exactly. (2) The bracketed sentence '[Ha pedig felmentéseket adtak világi érdekek biztosítása végett, mennyivel inkább illik azokat a lelkek szorongattatása miatt megadni]' correctly renders '[Now, if dispensations have been granted for the sake of securing temporal interests, it is much more proper that they be granted on account of the distress of souls]' — the comparative theological argument (temporal vs. spiritual distress) is intact. (3) The three conditions for a valid vow ('lehetséges dologra kell vonatkoznia, hogy szabadnak kell lennie, önkéntes és megfontolt döntésből kell származnia') correctly renders 'it ought to be in a thing possible, that it ought to be free, and chosen spontaneously and deliberately.' (4) The argument about perpetual chastity not being in human power ('mennyiben áll az ember hatalmában az örök tisztaság') is preserved. (5) The canonical law point about age fifteen ('tizenöt éves kor előtt tett fogadalmakat') and insufficient judgment for deciding about 'perpetual life' ('örök élethivatásról') is accurately rendered. No doctrinal content has been lost, added, or distorted.

terminology_consistency.explanation: All relevant approved glossary terms are used correctly and consistently. Key terms checked: 'Dispens/Dispensation' → 'felmentés' (correct, matches glossary); 'Gelübde' → 'fogadalom' (correct); 'Römischer Pontifex/Papst' → 'római főpap' (correct, matches 'Römischer Pontifex / Papst -> római főpap / pápa'); 'Gewissen / Not der Seelen' → 'lelkek szorongattatása' (correct, matches glossary); 'Verbindlichkeit des Gelübdes' → 'fogadalom kötelező ereje' (correct); 'Keuschheit' → 'tisztaság' (correct); 'freiwillig und überlegt' → 'önkéntes és megfontolt' (correct, matches 'freiwillig und überlegt -> önkéntes és megfontolt'); 'Kanonisches Recht' → 'kánonjog' (correct); 'Gelübde vor dem rechten Alter' → 'tizenöt éves kor előtt tett fogadalmakat' (contextually correct). The term 'örök tisztaság' for 'perpetual chastity' aligns with the 'Keuschheit' → 'tisztaság' mapping with 'örök' for 'perpetual' (matching 'eternal -> örök'). 'Kánonjogi rendelkezés' for 'canonical laws' is a reasonable and consistent expansion. No inconsistencies detected.

clarity.explanation: The Hungarian translation is clear and well-structured throughout. The three-part argument (dispensations, nature of vows, canonical age) is presented in a logical and easily followable manner. Phrases like 'Másodszor, miért nagyítják fel ellenfeleink a fogadalom kötelező erejét vagy hatását' clearly signal the second argument. The conditional structure 'Ha pedig felmentéseket adtak... mennyivel inkább illik' is natural and immediately comprehensible. The final paragraph about the age of fifteen and judgment is clear: 'e kor előtt úgy tűnik, hogy a személyben nincs elegendő ítélőképesség arra, hogy az örök élethivatásról döntsön.' No passages require re-reading to understand.

naturalness.explanation: The translation reads largely naturally in Hungarian theological prose. The formal, elevated register appropriate for confessional texts is well maintained. One minor trace of translation influence: 'tudniillik hogy annak lehetséges dologra kell vonatkoznia, hogy szabadnak kell lennie, önkéntes és megfontolt döntésből kell származnia' — the tripartite 'hogy... hogy... hogy' construction is slightly repetitive but is grammatically correct and comprehensible in Hungarian. Also 'Ifjú hajadonokat és ifjakat' is a stylistically appropriate but slightly formal rendering of 'Young maidens and men.' Overall the text reads as natural Hungarian theological prose with only minor traces of the translation process.

suggestions: The tripartite condition clause beginning with 'tudniillik hogy annak...' could be slightly smoothed for readability by restructuring as a bulleted or colon-introduced list in contexts where formatting allows, though this is a minor stylistic point for the current prose format.

The rendering of 'Most canonical laws' as 'A legtöbb kánonjogi rendelkezés' is accurate and natural; no change needed, but reviewers should note that 'kánonjogi rendelkezés' slightly expands 'canonical laws' — 'kánonok' alone (as in the glossary) could also work and would be more concise.

The phrase 'örök élethivatásról döntsön' (to decide about a perpetual life/vocation) is a reasonable rendering of 'decide concerning a perpetual life,' but consider whether 'élethivatás' (life vocation) fully captures the sense of 'perpetual life' as a state of existence/commitment — 'örök életre vonatkozó döntés' or 'örökre szóló életállapotról' might be slightly more precise, though the current rendering is acceptable.

The phrase 'az örök élethivatásról döntsön' could be slightly rephrased as 'az egész életre szóló fogadalomról döntsön' or 'az örökös szerzetesi életről határozzon' to more closely capture 'a perpetual life' in the context of monastic vows, though the current rendering is not doctrinally incorrect.

Consider whether 'kánonjogi rendelkezés' (for 'canonical laws') is the most consistent rendering with the approved glossary entry 'Kanonisches Recht/Kanones → egyházjog / kánonok'; a rendering like 'A legtöbb egyházi kánon' might align more closely with standard glossary usage.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Die römischen Bischöfe jedoch beurteilten weise, dass bei dieser Verpflichtung Nachsicht gezeigt werden müsse, und daher lesen wir, dass sie oft Befreiungen von Gelübden gewährten. Der Fall des Königs von Aragonien, der aus dem Kloster zurückgerufen wurde, ist wohlbekannt, und auch aus unserer eigenen Zeit gibt es Beispiele. [Wenn aber Befreiungen zur Sicherung weltlicher Interessen gewährt wurden, um wieviel mehr ziemt es sich, sie wegen der Bedrängnis der Seelen zu gewähren.]

Zweitens, warum vergrößern unsere Gegner die Bindungskraft oder Wirkung des Gelübdes, während sie gleichzeitig kein einziges Wort über die Natur des Gelübdes selbst sagen, nämlich dass es sich auf etwas Mögliches beziehen muss, dass es frei sein muss, aus einer freiwilligen und wohlerwogenen Entscheidung hervorgehen muss? Doch es ist nicht unbekannt, inwieweit es in der Macht des Menschen steht, ewige Keuschheit zu halten. Und wie wenige sind, die das Gelübde freiwillig und wohlerwogen abgelegt haben! Junge Mädchen und Jünglinge werden, bevor sie urteilsfähig sind, überredet, ja mitunter gezwungen, das Gelübde abzulegen. Daher ist es nicht billig, so streng an der Bindungskraft festzuhalten, da jedermann anerkennt, dass es gegen die Natur des Gelübdes ist, wenn es nicht aus freiwilligem und wohlerwogenem Handeln getan wird.

Die meisten kanonischrechtlichen Bestimmungen machen vor dem fünfzehnten Lebensjahr abgelegte Gelübde ungültig; denn vor diesem Alter scheint es, dass in der Person nicht ausreichend Urteilsvermögen vorhanden ist, um über den ewigen Lebensberuf zu entscheiden.

differences.original_phrase: the Roman Pontiffs have prudently judged
differences.back_translated_phrase: Die römischen Bischöfe jedoch beurteilten weise
differences.explanation: The original says 'Roman Pontiffs' (Päpste), a specific title for the Bishop of Rome as head of the Roman Catholic Church. The back-translation renders this as 'römischen Bischöfe' (Roman Bishops), which is a broader and less precise term. In a document discussing church authority and ecclesiastical practice, the distinction between 'Pontiffs' (popes) and generic 'bishops' is theologically and historically significant.

differences.original_phrase: leniency is to be observed in this obligation
differences.back_translated_phrase: dass bei dieser Verpflichtung Nachsicht gezeigt werden müsse
differences.explanation: The meaning is essentially the same. 'Nachsicht' (forbearance/indulgence) is a close synonym for leniency. No doctrinal shift.

differences.original_phrase: Most canonical laws rescind vows made before the age of fifteen
differences.back_translated_phrase: Die meisten kanonischrechtlichen Bestimmungen machen vor dem fünfzehnten Lebensjahr abgelegte Gelübde ungültig
differences.explanation: 'Rescind' and 'make invalid/null' (ungültig machen) are functionally equivalent in legal and canonical contexts. No meaningful doctrinal difference.

differences.original_phrase: to decide concerning a perpetual life
differences.back_translated_phrase: um über den ewigen Lebensberuf zu entscheiden
differences.explanation: The back-translation adds the nuance of 'vocation' (Lebensberuf) which slightly narrows 'perpetual life' to 'a lifelong vocation.' This is a minor interpretive addition but does not substantially change the meaning in context.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is well preserved. The most notable difference is the rendering of 'Roman Pontiffs' as 'Roman Bishops,' which is a moderate issue in a context discussing papal authority and dispensation power. All other differences are minor stylistic or synonym-level variations. The theological argument about the conditions for valid vows, the improper pressure placed on the young, and the canonical age threshold is faithfully conveyed.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation preserves the core doctrinal content well. Key theological claims are faithfully rendered: the argument that monastic vows are void because human-invented worship without God's command is wicked (Matt. 15:9), justification by faith alone, and the condemnation of monastic teaching that human services merit grace. However, there is one notable issue: the source says 'righteousness is not to be sought from our own observances and acts of worship, devised by men' — the translation renders this as 'az igazságot nem a magunk emberi cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' with a slash between 'cselekedetek' and 'obszervenciáiból', which looks like an editorial note or untranslated alternative rather than polished text. The Augustine reference (XXVII. Quaest. I, Cap. Nuptiarum) is preserved correctly. The Matt. 15:9 quotation follows a Hungarian biblical text rendering ('Pedig hiába tisztelnek engem, ha oly tudományokat tanítanak, amelyek embereknek parancsolatai') which is slightly expanded from the source's 'In vain do they worship Me with the commandments of men' but reflects standard Hungarian Bible translation. The final paragraph correctly states that monks taught that human-made services 'satisfy for sins and merit grace and justification' — rendered accurately as 'elégtételt adnak a bűnökért, és kiérdemlik a kegyelmet és a megigazulást.'

terminology_consistency.explanation: Most approved glossary terms are used correctly: 'fogadalmak' for vows (Gelübde), 'megigazulás' for justification (Rechtfertigung), 'kegyelem' for grace (Gnade), 'hit' for faith (Glaube), 'elégtétel' for satisfaction (Genugtuung), 'bűnök' for sins (Sünden), 'kolostorok' for monasteries (Klöster), 'istentisztelet' for service of God (Gottesdienst), 'Isten parancsa' for commandment of God. The term 'tanítóink' for 'our teachers' matches the glossary entry 'Lehrer / unsere Lehrer -> tanítók / a mi tanítóink'. However, the unresolved 'cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' (with a slash) for 'observances and acts of worship' is inconsistent — the glossary maps 'menschliche Werke/Observanzen -> emberi cselekedetek/obszervenciák' with a slash as an alternative, but this slash appears in the final translation text itself, which is not appropriate for a published translation. 'Szerzetesek' for 'monks' is consistent with the glossary ('Mönche -> szerzetesek'). Overall terminology is well-handled with only the one unresolved slash issue.

clarity.explanation: The translation is generally clear and readable. The argument flows logically from the canonical age discussion to Augustine's authority to the theological argument about man-made worship. The Károli Bible quotation for Matt. 15:9 ('Pedig hiába tisztelnek engem, ha oly tudományokat tanítanak, amelyek embereknek parancsolatai') is well-known to Hungarian readers. The main clarity issue is 'cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' — the slash in the middle of a sentence is jarring and would confuse a reader: 'a magunk emberi cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból és istentiszteleti cselekményeiből kell keresni.' This reads awkwardly and requires re-reading. Otherwise the text is understandable on first reading.

naturalness.explanation: The translation has a formal, elevated register appropriate for confessional theological prose, and much of it reads naturally. However, several constructions feel translated rather than native: 'megmutatván, hogy azok érvénytelenek' (using an archaic participial construction) is acceptable in formal theological Hungarian but slightly stiff. The phrase 'a magunk emberi cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból és istentiszteleti cselekményeiből kell keresni' is particularly unnatural due to the slash and the awkward genitive construction. 'Isten Krisztus érdeméért kegyelmébe fogadja őket' is natural and well-formed. The opening sentences read naturally. Overall, the slash issue and a few syntactic constructions prevent a fully natural reading experience.

critical_errors: The phrase 'a magunk emberi cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' contains an unresolved slash between two translation alternatives ('cselekedetek' and 'obszervenciáiból'), indicating the translator left an editorial choice unfinished. This is not a doctrinal error per se, but it renders that clause unclear and unpublishable as-is.

The unresolved 'cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' slash in the body of the translation is an editorial error — not a doctrinal error, but it must be resolved before publication as it makes the text unpublishable in its current form.
suggestions: The Matt. 15:9 quotation could be checked against the standard Hungarian Protestant Bible (Károli) to ensure consistency with what Hungarian Lutheran congregations would recognize.

Resolve the unresolved slash in 'cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' — choose one term or combine them naturally, e.g., 'emberi cselekedetekből és obszervenciákból' or simply 'emberi cselekedetekből és istentiszteleti gyakorlatokból'.

Resolve the 'cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból' slash by choosing one term or rephrasing: based on the glossary, 'emberi cselekedetek és obszervenciák' would be consistent, or simply 'emberi cselekedetek és istentiszteleti formák' — but a single clean choice must be made.

The participial 'megmutatván' could be replaced with a finite clause 'megmutatva' or 'annak megmutatására' for slightly more natural modern theological Hungarian, though the current form is not wrong.

Consider revising 'a magunk emberi cselekedetek/obszervenciáiból és istentiszteleti cselekményeiből kell keresni' to something like 'a magunk által kitalált cselekedetekből és istentiszteleti formákból kell keresni' for improved naturalness.
Consider replacing 'megmutatván' with 'megmutatva' or restructuring the sentence to avoid the archaic participial form, for slightly more natural modern Hungarian.

back_translation.back_translated_text:
Ein anderer Kanon, der menschlichen Schwachheit mehr nachgebend, fügt noch einige Jahre hinzu; denn er verbietet, vor dem achtzehnten Lebensjahr Gelübde abzulegen. Aber welchem dieser beiden Kanones sollen wir folgen? Die meisten haben eine Entschuldigung für das Verlassen der Klöster, da die Mehrheit das Gelübde vor Erreichen dieses Alters abgelegt hat.

Schließlich, auch wenn man den Bruch des Gelübdes tadeln könnte, folgt daraus nicht sogleich, dass die Ehe solcher Personen aufgelöst werden müsste. Denn Augustinus leugnet, dass sie aufgelöst werden müssten (XXVII. Quaest. I, Cap. Nuptiarum); und seine Autorität darf nicht leichtfertig beiseitegestellt werden, obwohl andere Menschen später anders geurteilt haben.

Obwohl es aber scheint, dass Gottes Gebot über die Ehe sehr viele von ihren Gelübden entbindet, bringen unsere Lehrer dennoch ein weiteres Argument bezüglich der Gelübde vor, indem sie zeigen, dass diese ungültig sind. Denn jeder Gottesdienst, den Menschen ohne Gottes Gebot eingesetzt und gewählt haben, um die Rechtfertigung und die Gnade zu verdienen, ist gottlos; wie Christus sagt, Matthäus 15,9: Sie ehren mich aber vergeblich, da sie Lehren lehren, die Menschengebote sind. Und Paulus lehrt überall, dass die Gerechtigkeit nicht aus unseren eigenen menschlichen Werken/Observanzen und Gottesdiensthandlungen gesucht werden soll, die Menschen erdacht haben, sondern dass sie durch den Glauben denen zuteilwird, die glauben, dass Gott sie um Christi Verdienst willen in seine Gnade aufnimmt.

Es ist aber offenkundig, dass die Mönche gelehrt haben, dass von Menschen erdachte Gottesdiensthandlungen für Sünden Genugtuung leisten und Gnade und Rechtfertigung verdienen.

differences.original_phrase: most of them have taken the vows before they reached these ages
differences.back_translated_phrase: die Mehrheit das Gelübde vor Erreichen dieses Alters abgelegt hat
differences.explanation: The original refers to 'these ages' (plural, referencing both canons' age thresholds), while the back-translation uses 'dieses Alters' (singular 'this age'). This is a very minor nuance and does not affect doctrinal meaning.

differences.original_phrase: to merit justification and grace
differences.back_translated_phrase: um die Rechtfertigung und die Gnade zu verdienen
differences.explanation: The order is reversed (justification before grace) compared to the original ('grace' comes after 'justification'). Actually the original says 'justification and grace' — the back-translation preserves this order. No meaningful difference.

differences.original_phrase: In vain do they worship Me with the commandments of men
differences.back_translated_phrase: Sie ehren mich aber vergeblich, da sie Lehren lehren, die Menschengebote sind
differences.explanation: The back-translation renders a slightly expanded form of Matthew 15:9, adding 'da sie Lehren lehren, die Menschengebote sind' (as they teach teachings that are human commandments). This is a fuller quotation of the verse and does not change the theological point being made.

differences.original_phrase: it comes by faith to those who believe that they are received by God into grace for Christ's sake
differences.back_translated_phrase: sie durch den Glauben denen zuteilwird, die glauben, dass Gott sie um Christi Verdienst willen in seine Gnade aufnimmt
differences.explanation: The back-translation adds 'Verdienst' (merit/merit's sake) — 'um Christi Verdienst willen' — whereas the original simply says 'for Christ's sake.' This slightly elaborates the basis of grace but does not alter the doctrinal substance; the meaning that God receives believers into grace on account of Christ is fully preserved.

differences.original_phrase: services of man's making satisfy for sins and merit grace and justification
differences.back_translated_phrase: von Menschen erdachte Gottesdiensthandlungen für Sünden Genugtuung leisten und Gnade und Rechtfertigung verdienen
differences.explanation: The back-translation preserves all key terms: human-devised worship acts, satisfaction for sins, meriting grace and justification. The phrasing is slightly different but the full doctrinal content is intact.

comparison.overall_assessment: The core doctrinal meaning is fully preserved across both texts. All major theological claims — the invalidity of vows made before canonical age, Augustine's position on marriage dissolution, the condemnation of human-devised worship as a means of justification, the Lutheran insistence on faith alone as the path to grace, and the critique of monastic teaching on satisfaction — are accurately represented in the back-translation. The differences noted are minor stylistic or paraphrastic variations with no doctrinal consequence.

doctrinal_accuracy.explanation: The translation preserves all doctrinal content with precision. Key theological claims are faithfully rendered: 'to detract from the glory of Christ' → 'Krisztus dicsőségének csorbítása'; 'righteousness of faith' → 'hit általi igazság'; 'wicked services' → 'istentelen szolgálatok'; 'void' → 'érvénytelenek'; 'Christ is become of no effect' → 'Krisztus hatástalanná lett'; 'fallen from grace' → 'kegyelemből kiestek'; 'justification to vows' → 'megigazulást fogadalmaknak tulajdonítják'; 'merited forgiveness of sins' → 'bűnbocsánatot érdemeltek ki'; 'state of Christian perfection' → 'keresztény tökéletesség állapota'; 'assigning justification to works' → 'megigazulásnak a cselekedetekhez való hozzárendelése'; 'service devised by men, without the commandment of God' → 'emberek által kitalált istentisztelet... Isten parancsa nélkül'. The condemnation of monastic merit theology, the Galatians 5:4 citation, and the conclusion about the Church offense are all accurately preserved.
